channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Global Economy Faces Long-Term Strain from Middle East Conflict and Energy Disruptions
item.description: Di International Monetary Fund (IMF), World Bank, and International Energy Agency (IEA) don yan say di wahala wey dey go on for Middle East go continue dey affect di whole world economy. Di wahala don...

item.title: Catholic leaders around the world back Pope Leo XIV’s peace appeal after Trump’s criticism
item.description: Religious leaders and organizations across the Philippines and beyond have expressed strong support for Pope Leo XIV after the pontiff faced criticism from US President Donald Trump, who accused him o...

item.title: Trump and Pope Leo XIV: Dispute over Politics and AI Image Sparks Controversy
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump criticised Pope Leo XIV after viewing a television segment where the pontiff expressed disapproval of the Iran war and Trump's immigration policies. Trump claimed the ...

item.title: P.E.I. Expands Healthcare Access by Hiring Additional Nurse Practitioners Amid Family Doctor Shortage
item.description: Prince Edward Island (P.E.I.) is addressing a significant healthcare challenge as thousands of residents remain without access to a family doctor. In response to growing demand and long wait times, th...

item.title: Benue Attack: 80-Year-Old Woman Mourns Loss of Three Children, Communities Devastated
item.description: Asups! Benue State don experience another serious attack from suspected herdsmen. Residents of Agana and Mbatsaida communities dey for serious pain and devastation after the attack wey happen on April...

item.title: Uttar Pradesh government revises minimum wage rates following industrial protests in Noida
item.description: The Government of Uttar Pradesh has announced a state-wide revision of minimum wages for workers across all employment categories, following recent unrest in Noida’s industrial areas. The decision com...

item.title: Solihull man sentenced to five years for injuring girlfriend in tower block incident
item.description: A man from Solihull has been sentenced to five years in prison after throwing his girlfriend from the fourth floor of a tower block, leaving her with life-changing injuries. Jordan Herring, aged 22, w...

item.title: Two MBA students die due to drug overdose at Mumbai concert venue
item.description: Tragic news has emerged from Mumbai where two MBA students lost their lives following a drug overdose during a concert at the NESCO exhibition centre. The young men, reportedly in their early twenties...

item.title: James Hird advises Zak Butters to appeal tribunal ruling if he maintains innocence
item.description: James Hird, the former AFL star, has publicly encouraged Port Adelaide player Zak Butters to appeal a tribunal decision if he insists that he did not make the statements for which he has been accused....

item.title: Punjab Assembly approves life imprisonment for sacrilege of Guru Granth Sahib
item.description: The Punjab Assembly has unanimously passed the 2026 Jaagat Jot Sri Guru Granth Sahib Satkar Amendment Bill, introducing stricter measures including life imprisonment for acts of sacrilege against the ...

item.title: U.S. Military Strike Kills Two Suspected Drug Traffickers in Eastern Pacific
item.description: Two individuals were killed in a U.S. military operation targeting a vessel allegedly involved in drug smuggling in the eastern Pacific, according to U.S. Southern Command. The strike was ordered by M...

item.title: PM Briefs President on US-Iran Dialogue and Upcoming Diplomatic Visits
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif met with President Asif Ali Zardari on Tuesday to provide a detailed briefing regarding the ongoing US-Iran talks and his scheduled visits to Saudi Arabia and Turkiye. Pa...

item.title: Scottish ministers criticised for failing to disclose collapse of £100m nature restoration investment deal
item.description: A £100 million private investment initiative aimed at supporting nature restoration projects in Scotland has collapsed without disclosure to the Scottish Parliament, prompting criticism of government ...

item.title: Twice-Yearly Injection Shows Promise for Controlling Resistant High Blood Pressure
item.description: A recent global clinical trial led by Queen Mary University of London has revealed that a new injectable drug, zilebesiran, may significantly improve blood pressure management. The study, published in...

item.title: Balikatan 2026 expands to 17,000 troops with seven-country participation amid regional tensions
item.description: Balikatan 2026 is set to become the largest iteration of the annual joint military exercises between the Philippines and the United States, involving more than 17,000 troops from seven countries. The ...

item.title: Novo Nordisk teams up with OpenAI to enhance drug research and operations
item.description: Danish pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk is collaborating with OpenAI to integrate artificial intelligence across its entire business. The partnership aims to improve drug discovery, manufacturing e...

item.title: Union Government Proposes Increase of Lok Sabha Seats to 850 with Updated Delimitation
item.description: The Union government of India is set to introduce legislation to revise the delimitation of Lok Sabha constituencies, which would raise the total seats in the Lower House from 543 to 850. Of these, 81...

item.title: Jaishankar holds discussion with Israeli FM on West Asia developments
item.description: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar of India held a telephonic discussion with Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar on Tuesday. According to Jaishankar, the conversation covered multiple aspects ...

item.title: AFN Names 24 Athletes to Represent Nigeria at 2026 World Athletics Relays in Gaborone
item.description: The Athletics Federation of Nigeria don select 24 top athletes wey go represent Naija for six relay races during the 2026 World Athletics Relays wey go hold for Gaborone from 2nd to 3rd May. Di AFN Te...

item.title: JCB heir warns inheritance tax reforms could force UK operations to relocate
item.description: Jo Bamford, heir to the JCB manufacturing empire, has voiced concerns over the UK Government's inheritance tax reforms, suggesting that they could compel the company to move its operations to the Unit...
